questions.question: Research on the testing effect has shown that:

options.text: Rereading notes is more effective than self-testing for long-term retention
Testing is only useful as an assessment tool and does not enhance learning
Actively retrieving information from memory through practice tests strengthens long-term retention
Students perform best when they avoid any form of testing until the final exam

questions.explanation: The testing effect (retrieval practice) is one of the most well-supported findings in memory research. Actively retrieving information from memory strengthens the memory trace and makes future retrieval easier, producing significantly better long-term retention than passive review strategies like rereading.

questions.question: A student memorizes a list of 20 historical dates by creating a vivid mental image of each event placed at a specific location along their walk to school. This student is using:
questions.explanation: The method of loci (memory palace technique) involves mentally placing items to be remembered at specific locations along a familiar route. To recall the items, the person mentally walks through the route and 'sees' each item at its designated location, leveraging the brain's strong spatial memory system.
